<thought>
In my current view, the cell directly in front of me is a wall (#), so I can’t move forward. The goal (G) is in the far right of the visible area, and the safest way to start is to change direction to avoid the wall. I’ll turn left to face the open floor cell to my left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I can see my position at row 07 column D (marked ^). The only visible safe cell adjacent is to the right at row 07 column E (.). The cell in front is a wall (#) at row 06 column D, so moving forward is not possible. I should turn right to face the open cell at (row 07, column E) and then move next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
From my position (row 07 column D), the cell in front is row 06 column D which is '.' (safe). The goal 'G' is visible at row 05 column F, so I should move forward one step to row 06 column D to get closer and then continue navigating around lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD

<thought>

<thought>

To reach the goal, I need to go left around the lava: turning left will face row 07 column C, which is a safe floor cell '.'.

<thought>
In my local view, I can see lava `L` immediately to my right (row 07 column E) and a wall `#` to my left (row 06 column C). The only safe forward cell is `.` (row 06 column D) if I face it. Currently I am oriented toward row 07 column D (shown as `^` at row 07 column D), and forward would hit the wall at row 06 column D or go into an unsafe area. To reach the safe `.` at row 06 column D, I need to turn to face it directly; from my current orientation, turning right will align me with that open cell. 
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT

<thought>

Turning left would face row 07 column C (column C), which is floor (.) and avoids lava; from there I can approach the goal without stepping into L.
</thought>
<action>

</thought>
<action>

<thought>

<action>
